# scaffold-internal.txt — tracked files intentionally NOT synced to consumers.
#
# The inverse of .github/scaffold-files.txt (the manifest of files that DO
# sync). Together they must classify every tracked file: CI
# (scripts/check-resolvable.mjs -> phaseManifestCompleteness) fails when a
# tracked file appears in neither list. That is how we stop "added a file but
# forgot to add it to the manifest" regressions — the default becomes "ship it
# unless it is explicitly held back here."
#
# This file is itself internal, so its presence also marks "this is the
# scaffold repo": the completeness check is skipped in consumer repos, which
# never receive this list.
#
# Syntax mirrors .scaffold-keep: exact path, dir prefix, or * glob (* spans /).

# This list + repo metadata (consumers keep their own)
.github/scaffold-internal.txt
.github/scaffold-sync-sha
.gitignore
LICENSE
README.md
package.json

# Scaffold's own CI (consumers get sync-scaffold.yml + quality.yml instead)
.github/workflows/ci.yml
.github/workflows/release.yml

# Session checkpoints
.pause

# Feature artifacts live in per-feature subfolders; top-level docs/*.md do ship
# (e.g. agent-authoring-requirements.md, skills.md) — except maintainer-only ones
docs/*/**
docs/branch-versioning.md

# Author-side sync engine shim (into tools/sync/**, also internal). Distinct
# from the consumer pull path bin/sync-from-scaffold.sh, which does ship.
# (globalize-skill.sh / install-skills.sh / repo-bound-skills.txt ship: they
# fix the @import dangle when globalizing a synced stub, which consumers hit too.)
bin/sync

# Scaffold's own dev/test scripts
scripts/compute-bump.*
scripts/test-*.sh

# Scaffold-internal tooling (not part of the consumer payload)
tools/README.md
tools/hoist-skill/test-integration
tools/sync
